summary: Builder.io/Qwik-themed package exfiltrates authenticated pendo.io session data to webhook.site
details: The package's main entry (index.js) executes browser DOM code that injects a hidden iframe pointed at pendo.io with builder.frameEditing=true, then posts a crafted 'builder.patchUpdates' message containing a payload script. The injected payload performs a credentialed fetch against https://novus-api.pendo.io/pendo/app (sending the visiting user's pendo.io cookies) and exfiltrates the base64-encoded response in chunks to https://webhook.site/d4d74967-cd7f-4b43-86f0-944e71ac7c97 via Image() pixel requests. The exploit targets ~40 hardcoded Builder.io content IDs (builder-<uuid>) and abuses the Builder.io postMessage patch protocol. The package name 'npm-builderio-qwik-poc' impersonates the Builder.io + Qwik ecosystem to lure developers integrating those tools. Any consumer application that loads this package in a browser will leak authenticated pendo.io API responses for the visiting user to the attacker's collector.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
